Current decode path
Has 6 steps, with lots of overhead in memory copy and small kernel launch/compute.
mixed_qkv [bs, qkv_dim]

    ▼ causal_conv1d_update()
mixed_qkv [bs, qkv_dim]           ← conv1d update

vllm.title: [CI] Fix GPU memory leak when RemoteOpenAIServer fails to start in __init__
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When RemoteOpenAIServer.__init__ raises (e.g. health check timeout), __exit__ is never called by Python's with statement, leaking the server + EngineCore subprocesses and their GPU memory. Every subsequent test then OOMs.
Extracted cleanup into _shutdown() called from both __exit__ and __init__'s ex

vllm.title: [Perf] Optimize top-k search in apply_top_k_top_p_triton sampler
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Fix min_larger in top-k ternary search to compute min of values above the pivot instead of the global min. This makes the termination condition converge correctly (~3-5 iterations instead of hitting the 18-iteration cap).
Fuse the num_min_larger second pass into the first pass for the top-k

vllm.title: [CI] Split Distributed Tests (4 GPUs) and Kernel MoE tests
vllm.author: avinashsingh77
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Split the following CI jobs

Kernels MoE Test: parallelism: 2 → parallelism: 5, timeout 60m → 25m
Distributed (2 GPUs): Split to 3 Jobs(DP Tests, Compile+RPC, Torchrun+Shutdown), 20m each
API Server 1: Added parallelism: 5 with --shard-id/--num-shards, timeout 130m → 25m

Test Plan

 Verify all new

the public API torch.cpu._is_amx_tile_supported()
PyTorch 2.11 removed/renamed these private CPU

The --calculate-kv-scales feature dynamically calibrates FP8 KV cache scales using the first batc

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Rescale NVFP4 weight scales to fix BF16 dequant underflow
vllm.author: ricky-chaoju
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The Marlin NVFP4 kernel converts weight scales to a special S0E5M3 format by multiplying by 2^7, assuming the MSB is always 1 for non-zero values. However, some models (e.g., those with global_scale=1) produce small scale values that violate this assumption. On BF16, the exponent widening in

vllm.title: [Models] Cohere Transcribe
vllm.author: ekagra-ranjan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add Cohere Transcribe to vLLM.
It uses all the variable length encoder changes that were made in the last few months to vLLM scheduler to relax the constraint of having fixed length encoder inputs (#31058, #29268, #29278).
This model doen't have a HF impl yet so we added the HF processor, to

vllm.title: [Model] Add ColQwen3.5 4.5B support
vllm.author: athrael-soju
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add support for ColQwen3.5 late interaction model for multi-modal document retrieval and reranking.
ColQwen3.5 extends the ColPali architecture using the Qwen3.5 hybrid backbone (linear + full attention). It produces per-token L2-normalized embeddings and uses ColBERT-style MaxSim scoring fo

vllm.title: Make KV connector metadata build overridable via plugin
vllm.author: shinoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Move self.connector.build_connector_meta step in scheduler to its own method so it can be overridden (e.g. via plugin). This gives us the flexibility of having each plugin pass additional info via the SchedulerOutput as necessary without having to add fields to the struct for everyone else.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Fix worker startup OOM on ROCm by skipping unreliable cudagraph memory profiling
vllm.author: JartX

Problem
On ROCm/HIP platforms, vLLM fails to start with:
ValueError: Free memory on device cuda:0 (1.33/23.98 GiB) on startup is less
than desired GPU memory utilization (0.95, 22.79 GiB).

This was introduced by the pr
[UX][Startup] Account for CUDA graphs during memory profiling
#30515), which rem

vllm.title: [ROCm] Validate block_size for explicitly selected attention backends
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #36274 stripped block_size from attn_selector_config before backend validation in get_attn_backend_cls, which was correct for auto-selection (block_size may not be finalized at that point). However, this also bypassed block_size validation for explicitly user-selected backends, breaking the contract

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add non-gated support for NVFP4 CUTLASS MoE

Purpose

Add no_act_and_mul support to CutlassExpertsFp4, enabling non-gated MoE models (e.g. Nemotron-Nano) to use the CUTLASS nvfp4 kernel via --moe-backend=cutlass
Add is_gated parameter to get_cutlass_moe_mm_data so the CUDA kernel computes the correct first GEMM problem size (n vs 2*n) without

vllm.title: [ROCm][Quantization] add fp8xfp8 attn support for rocm_aiter_unified_attn
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As a follow-up of #35316,
This PR adds support for fp8xfp8 attention for rocm_aiter_unified_attn
For fp8xfp8 computation, the aiter triton kernel expects Q to be in fp8 as well, otherwise it descales it to fp32. (LINK). However, Q scales are not supported in the kernel (LINK)
To handle this, we abso

vllm.title: [Kernel][Helion] [16/N] Refactor register_kernel API to be more Dynamo-friendly
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Part of initialization logic of Helion kernels (like reading GPU platform info, reading config files from disk) are not Dynamo-friendly.
To make vLLM-Helion torch.compile-friendly, this PR moves that part of logic out into __init__ phase of HelionKernelWrapper. This forces __init__ to also take a ma

vllm.title: [Performance] Add --enable-ep-weight-filter CLI option
vllm.author: esmeetu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add --enable-ep-weight-filter opt-in CLI flag to skip non-local expert weights during model loading when EP is active
Makes the EP weight filter from #37136 an explicit opt-in feature
No behavior change without the flag
Regression mentioned in #37322

Usage
vllm serve model \
  --enable-exp

Follows the same skip pattern used in test_flashinfer.py

vllm.title: [kv_offload+HMA][0/N]: Support block-level preemption handling
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR changes the handle_preemptions connector API function to support handling of arbitrary events via KVConnectorMetadata.
Specifically, this will allow handling of sliding-window layer blocks which can be evicted from the GPU KV cache while still being saved by a connector.
